Value: 219","CreationDate":"2025-12-12","SUBJECT":"EDUCATION, WALES","CATEGORY":"EDUCATION, WALES","SUMMARY":"These Regulations apply to local authorities and governing bodies that provide food and drink on a school day to registered pupils at maintained primary schools, whether the food and drink is provided on school premises or not. For the purposes of these Regulations, “maintained primary school” means a maintained school that provides primary education (whether or not it also provides other kinds of education). “Maintained school” means a community, foundation or voluntary school, a community special school or a pupil referral unit in Wales or a maintained nursery school as defined in regulation 2(1). Where a maintained primary school also provides secondary education, these Regulations will apply only to food and drink provided to registered pupils in year 6 and below.","":""}

Welsh Statutory Instruments 2025 No. 1343 (W. 219) Education, Wales The Healthy Eating in Schools (Nutritional Standards and Requirements) (Maintained Primary Schools) (Wales) Regulations 2025 Made 12 December 2025 Laid before Senedd Cymru 17 December 2025 Coming into force 31 October 2026 The Welsh Ministers make the following Regulations in exercise of the powers conferred upon them by sections 4(1), (2) and (8) and 10(2) and (3) of the Healthy Eating in Schools (Wales) Measure 2009( 1 ), and having taken steps to ascertain the views of pupils and consulted such other persons as they consider appropriate as required by section 4(11) of that Measure. Title and coming into force 1. —(1) The title of these Regulations is the Healthy Eating in Schools (Nutritional Standards and Requirements) (Maintained Primary Schools) (Wales) Regulations 2025. (2) These Regulations come into force on 31 October 2026. Interpretation 2. —(1) In these Regulations— “ the 1996 Act ” (“ Deddf 1996 ”) means the Education Act 1996( 2 ); “ boarding school ” (“ ysgol fyrddio ”) means a maintained primary school which has boarding pupils, whether or not it also has day pupils; “ compulsory school age ” (“ oedran ysgol gorfodol ”) has the meaning given to it by section 8 of the 1996 Act( 3 ); “ confectionery ” (“ melysion ”) means— (a) cereal bars; (b) chewing gum; (c) chocolate in any form; (d) any product containing chocolate or wholly or partially coated with chocolate; (e) any chocolate flavoured substance except cocoa powder used in sweetened baked products and desserts; (f) decorative toppings made from sugar or icings made from sugar; (g) processed or extruded fruit bars; (h) other non-chocolate confectionery (whether or not containing sugar) including mints; “ free sugars ” (“ siwgrau rhydd ”) means— (a) all monosaccharides and disaccharides added to foods, and (b) all sugars naturally present in honey, syrups and unsweetened fruit juice; “ fruit ” (“ ffrwythau ”) does not include fruit juice; “ fruit juice ” (“ sudd ffrwythau ”) means— (a) a product that complies with the specification in Schedule 2 (specification for fruit juice) to the Fruit Juices and Fruit Nectars (Wales) Regulations 2013( 4 ), or (b) a product that complies with the specification in Schedule 3 (specification for fruit juice from concentrate) to the Fruit Juices and Fruit Nectars (Wales) Regulations 2013; “ governing body ” (“ corff llywodraethu ”), in relation to a maintained primary school that is a pupil referral unit, includes the management committee of the pupil referral unit; “ infant pupil ” (“ disgybl o oedran babanod ”) means a registered pupil in a nursery class, a reception year, year 1 or year 2; “ junior pupil ” (“ disgybl iau ”) means a registered pupil in year 3, year 4, year 5 or year 6; “ local authority ” (“ awdurdod lleol ”) has the meaning given to “local authority in Wales” by section 579(1) of the 1996 Act( 5 ); “ maintained nursery school ” (“ ysgol feithrin a gynhelir ”) means a nursery school which is maintained by a local authority and is not a special school; “ maintained primary school ” (“ ysgol gynradd a gynhelir ”) means a maintained school that provides primary education (whether or not it also provides other kinds of education); “ maintained school ” (“ ysgol a gynhelir ”) means a community, foundation or voluntary school, a community special school or a pupil referral unit in Wales or a maintained nursery school; “ meat ” (“ cig ”) means the skeletal muscles of mammalian and bird species recognised as fit for human consumption with naturally included or adherent tissue but does not include mechanically separated meat; “ mechanically separated meat ” (“ cig a wahenir yn fecanyddol ”) has the meaning given in point 1.14 of Annex 1 to Regulation (EC) No 853/2004 of the European Parliament and of the Council laying down specific hygiene rules for food of animal origin( 6 ); “ medically prescribed dietary requirements ” (“ anghenion deietegol sydd wedi eu rhagnodi’n feddygol ”) means dietary requirements prescribed or recommended in writing for a pupil by— (a) a registered medical practitioner, or (b) a dietician who is registered in Part 4 of the register maintained under article 5 of the Health Professions Order 2001( 7 ); “ nursery class ” (“ dosbarth meithrin ”) means a class of pupils who are provided with education suitable for children who have not attained compulsory school age; “ plain plant-based drinks ” (“ diodydd plaen sydd wedi eu seilio ar blanhigion ”) means oat, soya or other plant-based drinks (but not including rice drinks), which— (a) are fortified with calcium, (b) are unflavoured, and (c) do not contain free sugars; “ primary education ” (“ addysg gynradd ”) has the meaning given to it by section 2(1) of the 1996 Act( 8 ); “ processed alternatives to meat and fish ” (“ dewisiadau amgen wedi eu prosesu yn lle cig a physgod ”) means industrially produced processed non-meat and non-fish food products that at
